title: The Mirror Test for Self-Recognition
script_summary: Exploring the mirror test reveals fascinating insights into animal self-recognition and the debates surrounding it.

segments.voiceover: Can animals recognize themselves in a mirror?
The mirror test is a fascinating experiment that reveals self-recognition in certain species.
Some animals, like great apes and dolphins, pass this test, suggesting a level of self-awareness.
But passing the mirror test isn’t a full sign of a theory of mind, just self-recognition.
This sparks debates in comparative psychology about consciousness and cognition in animals.
What do you think? Can self-awareness exist without a theory of mind? Comment below!
